name: Reverse Grip Lat Pulldown
cues: Chest up, drive elbows down toward your hips.
1-2s stretch pause at the top on the last rep.
bloodAndGutsNote: A Yates staple for the lower lats. Pull with the elbows, not the hands, and hold the fully stretched position before the working set's final reps.

name: T-Bar Row
cues: Hinge at the hips with a neutral spine.
Row the bar to the lower ribs, squeeze the shoulder blades.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates built his thickness on heavy rows. Use straps so grip never limits the back, and take the one all-out set to failure.

name: Iso-Lateral Row
cues: Full stretch at the front, slight torso twist.
Pause and squeeze at the contracted position.
bloodAndGutsNote: Machine rows let you push to failure safely without a spotter. First warm-up is very light to groove the movement.

name: Triceps Pushdown
bloodAndGutsNote: Great finisher; extend the failure set with a drop set or rest-pause.

name: Cable Biceps Curl
cues: Constant tension - don't fully relax at the bottom.

name: Back Extension (Weighted Hyperextension)
cues: Pure hip hinge, keep a neutral spine.
10s iso-hold at the end of the set.

name: Chest Fly (Machine)
cues: Feel the stretch across the chest.
Squeeze the pecs together at the front.
bloodAndGutsNote: Used as a pre-exhaust before pressing so the chest fails before the triceps.

name: Incline Bench Press (Smith Machine)
cues: Lower to the upper chest with control.
Drive up hard; the Smith path lets you push to failure solo.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates favored the Smith machine for pressing because the fixed path is safe to take beyond failure with forced reps.

name: Chest Press (Machine)
cues: Full press without locking out hard.

name: Preacher Curl (Barbell)
cues: Back of arms flat on the pad.
Don't bounce out of the bottom.
Control the stretch, hard squeeze at the top.

name: Behind the Back Cable Curl
cues: Stand slightly ahead of the low pulley so the arm is behind the torso.
Keep the elbow back to load the long head.

name: Leg Extension (Machine)
cues: Lock the hips down, control any swing.
10-15s iso-hold plus 2-3 partials on the last rep.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates used leg extensions to pre-exhaust the quads before hack squats or leg presses.

name: Lying Leg Curl (Machine)
cues: Slow the eccentric, no dropping the weight.

name: Seated Calf Raise
cues: 2-3s stretch at the bottom, 1s peak hold at the top.
Drop ~25% and rep to a burn to finish.

name: Hack Squat (Machine)
cues: Drive through mid-foot; keep constant tension.
bloodAndGutsNote: A safer alternative to the barbell squat that still lets you go to failure. Yates leaned on machines to overload the quads.

name: Leg Raise (Parallel Bars)
cues: Support on the elbows, back flat against the pad.
Raise with a posterior pelvic tilt, not just the hip flexors.

name: Romanian Deadlift (Barbell)
cues: Hinge at the hips, soft knees.
Bar tracks close to the shins.
2s stretch at the bottom, feel the hamstrings load.

name: Standing Leg Curl
cues: One leg at a time, hips still.

name: Leg Press (Horizontal)
cues: Control depth with the pelvis, don't let the low back round.
3-4 slow eccentrics to finish the set.

name: Standing Calf Raise (Machine)
cues: Rise onto the big toe, peak squeeze.

name: Decline Crunch
cues: Posterior pelvic tilt, no pulling on the neck.

name: Lateral Raise (Machine)
cues: Raise to shoulder height, no shrugging.

name: Dumbbell Shrug
cues: Hold the top squeeze for a second.

name: Rear Delt Reverse Fly (Machine)
cues: Sweep the arms wide, avoid using the traps.
Squeeze the rear delts at the back.

name: Weighted Triceps Dip
cues: Torso fairly upright to bias triceps.
Elbows tucked, press through the heel of the palm.

name: Overhead Triceps Extension (Cable)
cues: Split the rope at the bottom.
Keep the shoulders from drifting forward.

name: Treadmill
cues: Used for off-season conditioning, not to interfere with recovery.

name: Incline Barbell Press
cues: Lower to the upper chest, elbows ~45 degrees.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates opened his chest workout with incline pressing to prioritize the upper chest.

name: Incline Dumbbell Flye
cues: Deep stretch, don't overextend the shoulders.

name: Barbell Row (Bent-Over)
cues: Straps on so grip doesn't fail first.
bloodAndGutsNote: The signature Yates underhand-grip row that built his legendary back thickness.

name: Machine Pullover
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates used the Nautilus pullover as a lat pre-exhaust before rows and pulldowns.

name: Deadlift (Barbell)
cues: Push the floor away, bar close to the body.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates finished back day with heavy deadlifts for one brutal all-out set.

name: Seated Cable Row
cues: Pull to the navel, squeeze the shoulder blades.

name: Shoulder Press (Machine)
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates pressed on a machine so he could safely take shoulders to failure with forced reps.

name: Shoulder Press (Smith Machine)
cues: Press overhead in the fixed track.
Lower under control to the top of the chest.

name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
cues: Elbow braced on the inner thigh.

name: Lying Triceps Extension (Skull Crusher)
cues: Elbows pointed at the ceiling, kept still.
Lower to the forehead/behind the head.

name: Leg Press (45-Degree)
cues: Lower to a deep but safe range.
Push through the whole foot, no locking hard.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
cues: Lead with the elbows, slight lean forward.
